headline: Is Coca-Cola Paying More in Dividends Than It Can Afford?
summary: Coca-Cola (NYSE: KO) delivered its Q1 2026 dividend of $0.53 per share to shareholders on April 1, 2026, marking another milestone in one of the most unbroken dividend growth streaks in corporate America. The payment reflects a $0.02 per share increase over the prior quarterly rate of $0.51, extending Coca-Cola’s record of 63 consecutive years ... Is Coca-Cola Paying More in Dividends Than It Can Afford?

headline: CFOs On the Move: Week ending April 3
summary: Driscoll’s appoints a Coca-Cola exec as CFO, Indeed promotes an insider to finance chief and chip designer Broadcom hires an Alphabet exec as chief financial officer.
